title: S3: fence docs/specs read-only for NEW content, and flip the reading order to bundle-first
action: Two halves, both gated on bundleMode so an un-migrated host repo is unaffected (G1). PART A — THE FENCE (G2): add a chain check that FAILS when a file under docs/specs/ is neither (i) a pointer stub, nor (ii) reading-map.md, nor (iii) okf-profile.md. Today's tree is exactly: 10 stubs (advisor-protocol, decision-memory, doctrine-layer, feedback-digest, hook-runtime, onboarding, performance-log, verify-pipeline, workflow-state, worktree-parallelism), reading-map.md, okf-profile.md, and system-overview.md — a 7-line UNWRITTEN placeholder. CONFIRM the check is SILENT on all 13 before wiring it, and decide system-overview.md explicitly: it holds no content to migrate, so either allowlist it as a known placeholder WITH a reason, or leave it failing and fix it in this cell — do not let it be an accidental pass. Recognising a stub must be structural (its frontmatter carries migrated_to, per the shape the migration loop already writes), never a filename list that rots. The check MUST be inert when bundleMode is false — a host repo with no bundle keeps writing docs/specs/ freely, which is the whole point of G1. Pin it exact-path in MANDATORY_SUITES. PART B — READING ORDER (G4): update skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md (the State layer paragraph at ~line 69 and the Runtime Files line at ~185), skills/bee-planning/SKILL.md (bootstrap step 2), and skills/bee-hive/templates/AGENTS.block.md so the order is bundle -> decisions -> history: read docs/knowledge/areas/<area>/ first (the index names the concepts), docs/specs/ named ONLY as the read-only compatibility surface that resolves legacy anchor citations. Keep every instruction dual-mode: when there is no bundle the existing docs/specs guidance stands verbatim. AGENTS.md is RENDERED from the template block — edit the block, re-render, and keep both under 20480 bytes (currently 17939 / 17821; scripts/test_agents_budget.mjs enforces it). RED-FIRST: a fixture with a new non-stub prose file under docs/specs/ must fail the fence before the fence exists; a bundle-less fixture must stay silent.

truths: red evidence: the fence tests failed before implementation (verbatim)
the fence is SILENT on all 13 current docs/specs entries — enumerated in the trace with the verdict for each
system-overview.md is handled EXPLICITLY (allowlisted with a stated reason, or fixed) — never an accidental pass
a new non-stub prose file under docs/specs/ FAILS the fence — demonstrated, fixture removed
the fence is INERT in a bundle-less fixture — demonstrated
stub recognition is structural (migrated_to frontmatter), not a hardcoded filename list
AGENTS.md and the template block both stay under 20480 bytes — counts quoted
the reading order in bee-hive, bee-planning and the AGENTS block names the bundle first and docs/specs only as the compatibility surface, with the no-bundle guidance intact
prohibitions: never make the fence fire in a repo with no bundle (G1)
never recognise stubs by a filename list — it rots the moment an area is added
do not touch scribingTarget or the anti-fork gate — f3-3 owns them
never let AGENTS.md exceed its budget

trace.outcome: docs/specs/ fenced read-only for NEW content (structural stub recognition, inert with no bundle) and the reading order flipped to bundle-first in bee-hive, bee-planning and the AGENTS block

Before working in any area, the reading order is **spec -> decisions -> history**: read `docs/specs/<area>.md` (what the area does now) before its code\". AGENTS.md critical rule 9 carried: \"A settled rule, behavior, or value gets captured into `docs/specs/` the moment it settles\" — an instruction the new fence would itself refuse. Both are now bundle-first, with the no-bundle branch kept verbatim (G1)."
}
